title: dactylis glomerata nothovar. subhirsuta
intro: Dactylis glomerata nothovar. subhirsuta, also known as a variant of the common cockfoot or orchard grass, is a plant species that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor infections. The health benefits of Dactylis glomerata nothovar. subhirsuta include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area for relief from minor skin issues.
